title: Expose pagination as a complete iterator
description: Provide automatic page-following iteration in generated clients so callers traverse a whole collection without hand-managing cursors.
body: Use when an SDK wraps a list/collection endpoint that returns partial pages. Do not force every caller to read next-page tokens and loop manually, and do not silently return only the first page. Actions: surface both a raw page accessor and an auto-paginating iterator/async-iterable that follows the cursor, document the page size and ordering guarantees, and bound memory by streaming rather than buffering all pages. Validation signals include a contract test that iterates a multi-page fixture and a count matching the server total. Risk boundary: an auto-paginator that hides rate limits can hammer the API; record and respect server paging limits, and surface partial-failure rather than an infinite loop.
